issues.title: categoryConstants.ts duplicated between store and studio apps
issues.description: The entire `categoryConstants.ts` file — including the `CategoryTheme` interface, `buildTheme` helper, `CATEGORY_THEMES` record, and `getCategoryTheme` function — is copy-pasted identically between `apps/store/src/shared/domain/categoryConstants.ts` and `apps/studio/src/shared/domain/categoryConstants.ts`. The studio version even has a comment explicitly acknowledging it: 'mirrors store's CategoryTheme'. Both tests (`categoryConstants.test.ts`) also overlap significantly. The only difference is the import source for `ProductCategory`: store uses `@/shared/domain/CategoryTypes`, studio uses `shared/types`.
issues.recommendation: Move `CategoryTheme`, `buildTheme`, `CATEGORY_THEMES`, `getCategoryTheme` into `packages/shared` (since `ProductCategory` from `shared/types` is already there). Both apps can then import from the shared package. The `getCategoryColor` and `PRODUCT_CATEGORIES` helpers that are store-only can remain in the store.

issues.title: validateUuid and createRestPath duplicated between admin and payments API routes
issues.description: `validateUuid` and `createRestPath` are implemented independently in `apps/admin/src/app/api/admin/_shared/adminRest.ts` and `apps/payments/src/app/api/checkout/payment-methods/route.ts`. Both functions have identical logic (UUID regex validation, URLSearchParams builder). The UUID regex patterns differ slightly (admin uses version-constrained `[1-5]`, payments uses a looser pattern) but serve the same purpose. The admin version is already extracted to a shared file; the payments version re-implements it inline.
issues.recommendation: The payments route already imports `adminFetchJson` from `@/shared/infrastructure/adminRestClient`. Move `validateUuid` and `createRestPath` into that shared client (`apps/payments/src/shared/infrastructure/adminRestClient.ts`) and import them in the route handler, eliminating the inline re-implementations.

issues.title: getSupabaseConfig and auditQuery duplicated within admin app
issues.description: Within `apps/admin`, both `src/shared/infrastructure/recentActivityQueries.ts` and `src/features/audit/infrastructure/auditQueries.ts` define identical `getSupabaseConfig()` functions and near-identical `auditQuery()` functions. The constants `PARAM_ORDER`, `PARAM_SELECT`, and `ORDER_BY_TIMESTAMP_DESC` are also duplicated between the two files.
issues.recommendation: Extract `getSupabaseConfig`, `auditQuery`, and the shared constants (`PARAM_ORDER`, `PARAM_SELECT`, `ORDER_BY_TIMESTAMP_DESC`) into a new file at `apps/admin/src/shared/infrastructure/auditRestClient.ts`. Both query files can then import from that shared client.

issues.title: formatCOP/formatCop duplicated between studio and payments apps
issues.description: `apps/studio/src/features/products/presentation/components/ProductTableRow.tsx` defines a local `formatCOP` function using `Intl.NumberFormat('es-CO', { style: 'currency', currency: 'COP', ... })`. `apps/payments/src/shared/application/utils/formatCop.ts` exports an identical `formatCop` function. The studio version is private (not exported) and uses a slightly different config (`maximumFractionDigits: 0` explicitly). This is the same knowledge duplicated.
issues.recommendation: Move `formatCop` to `packages/shared` (since both studio and payments use it), or have the studio import `formatCop` from `apps/payments` is not correct — instead, extract to `packages/shared/src/utils/formatCop.ts` and import in both apps.

issues.title: parseTags duplicated within studio app
issues.description: Within `apps/studio`, `parseTags` (split by comma, trim, filter falsy) is defined twice: once in `src/features/products/application/hooks/useProductForm.ts` (named `parseTags`) and once in `src/features/products/presentation/components/inline-editor/InlineTagEditor.tsx` (also named `parseTags`). Both functions are identical.
issues.recommendation: Extract `parseTags` to `apps/studio/src/features/products/domain/utils.ts` (or the existing `domain/` layer) and import it in both places. This is a minor within-feature DRY violation.

issues.title: getItemName duplicated between OrderItemsList and ReceivedOrderCard
issues.description: Both `apps/payments/src/features/orders/presentation/components/OrderItemsList.tsx` and `apps/payments/src/features/received-orders/presentation/components/ReceivedOrderCard.tsx` define an identical `getItemName(item: OrderItem, locale: string): string` function with the same body: `i18nField(item.metadata, 'name', locale) || item.product_id`.
issues.recommendation: Extract `getItemName` to `apps/payments/src/shared/domain/orderUtils.ts` and import it in both components. Both orders and received-orders features deal with the same `OrderItem` type, so the shared domain level is appropriate.

issues.title: CartItem and SellerGroup redefined in payments/checkout with different shape than store/cart
issues.description: `apps/store/src/features/cart/domain/types.ts` exports `CartItem` and `SellerGroup`. `apps/payments/src/features/checkout/domain/types.ts` redefines both interfaces with a different (flattened/extended) shape. The payments `CartItem` has an explicit `[key: string]: unknown` index signature to accept the full product row from the cookie. The `SellerGroup` in payments adds `sellerName` and renames `subtotal` to `subtotalCop`. These are intentionally different shapes (the comment in payments acknowledges the cookie shape), so this is accepted architectural duplication, not a violation. Noting as suggestion for awareness.
issues.recommendation: Document clearly that `payments/checkout/domain/types.ts::CartItem` is intentionally a looser superset of `store/cart/domain/types.ts::CartItem` to accommodate cookie deserialization. If both apps evolve, consider sharing a base `CartItemBase` type in `packages/shared` and extending in each app. Low priority — the current approach is intentional and documented.

patterns.name: createWrapper test pattern
patterns.description: 26 test files across all apps define a near-identical `createWrapper()` function that wraps children in a `QueryClientProvider` with `retry: false`. This is an acceptable pattern for isolated hook tests (test code DRY exceptions apply per rules), but could benefit from a shared `createTestWrapper` utility in each app's `src/test/` setup file if it becomes burdensome.

patterns.name: MSWProvider wrapper pattern
patterns.description: Three apps (admin, store, payments) have identical `MSWProvider.tsx` wrapper files that delegate to `SharedMSWProvider` from `shared/providers`. This pattern is correct architecture (apps wire up their own mocks path) and is not a DRY violation.
